picks.title: Bruno Mars – The Romantic Tour
picks.blurb: GRAMMY-winning pop sensation brings his first full stadium tour to Amsterdam, with DJ Pee Wee and Victoria Monét.

picks.title: World Press Photo Exhibition 2026
picks.blurb: Award-winning photojournalism documenting global events; powerful images revealing world's most important stories and human moments.
picks.source: I amsterdam · Your Little Black Book

picks.title: The Moonwalkers: A Journey with Tom Hanks
picks.blurb: Immersive experience narrated by Tom Hanks: journey through Apollo moon missions with stunning projections and historical storytelling.
picks.why: Space exploration brought to vivid life

picks.title: Le Petit Bouillon d'Amsterdam
picks.blurb: Intimate French bistro with sunny terrace serving classic French cuisine and small bites.
picks.why: New French restaurant with sunny terrace

picks.title: Rembrandt & Life
picks.blurb: Hands-on exhibition exploring Rembrandt's craftsmanship, technique and tactics through masterclasses and interactive displays.
picks.why: Closing end of August; masterworks on final weeks

picks.title: Liefde op de Grachten – 30 Jaar Canal Parade
picks.blurb: Celebrate 30 years of Canal Parade through photos, art, personal stories showing how Amsterdam's Pride grew into global phenomenon.

picks.title: Hortus Botanicus
picks.blurb: One of the world’s oldest botanical gardens — a butterfly greenhouse and three-climate glasshouse to wander.
picks.source: De Hortus · Your Little Black Book

picks.title: Nara Nara
picks.blurb: New Egyptian restaurant by chef Said with shared dishes celebrating Cairo flavors and warmth.
picks.why: Authentic Egyptian dining with family vibe

picks.title: The Jollof Club
picks.blurb: West African cuisine by chef Nicolas Aquaa with focus on Ghana, Nigeria, and Senegal traditions.
picks.why: Modern West African dining with storytelling

picks.title: Kay Slice QUANZA at Openluchttheater Vondelpark
picks.blurb: Free outdoor performance in the park. Bring a picnic blanket, snacks and cooler for a summer evening.
picks.why: Free concert in iconic park setting

picks.title: Pllek — waterfront hang
picks.blurb: A cargo-container café-bar on the NDSM wharf — big windows onto the IJ, sofas, and a wood stove when it turns.
picks.why: Indoor seating · IJ views · cozy when grey

picks.title: Café Chris — oldest brown café
picks.blurb: Open since 1624: a creaky, golden-lit Jordaan brown café for a slow afternoon beer while it pours outside.

picks.title: Foodhallen
picks.blurb: An indoor food hall in a converted tram depot — 20-odd stalls, long tables and two bars under one roof.
picks.why: Indoor · grazing for a crowd · weather-proof

picks.title: L.A.V.A.
picks.blurb: Contemporary dance bringing together two radical worlds—choreography by Emio Greco and Roberto Zappalà. Part of Julidans festival.
picks.why: Julidans contemporary dance festival on now

picks.title: Guilty Pleasure Festival – Multigroove
picks.blurb: Nostalgic 90s/00s dance and hardcore festival with multiple stages across waterside recreation area.

picks.title: Guilty Pleasure presents: Busta Rhymes Live
picks.blurb: Hip-hop icon Busta Rhymes performs exclusive only-show in Netherlands/Belgium with festival lineup.

picks.title: Vunzige Deuntjes Festival
picks.blurb: 20,000 visitors, 6 stages, dancehall, hip-hop, house, disco with live acts and DJs across the forest.

picks.title: Batik, Beats & Bumbu
picks.blurb:

 Indonesia was a Dutch colony for over 350 years. After the Second World War, some 350,000 Dutch people of Indonesian descent came

picks.title: Julidans: Shiraz Armin Hokmi (IR)
picks.blurb:
A small step, and a world that slowly starts moving
 Shiraz is inspired by the legendary Shiraz Arts Festival (1967-1977) in southern Iran, where artists from a

picks.title: It's the end of the amusement phase
picks.blurb:
A sharp, overwhelming collage
 Three women dance as if they have landed in the middle of an endless marathon. Their bodies carry memories, history, and exhausti

picks.title: Transit
picks.blurb:
The Exhibition
 Rather than illustrating research outcomes, the exhibition brings contemporary art into dialogue with academic inquiry. Where research offers co

picks.title: Vondelpark Openluchttheater
picks.blurb:
In the heart of Amsterdam
 The first real Vondelpark festivals originated in the 1950s, and this continued to grow. Since 1974, the Vondelpark Open Air Theatre
picks.source: I amsterdam · Your Little Black Book

picks.title: VriendenLoterij Summer Concerts
picks.blurb:
Celebrate summer with music
 As in previous years, you can once again enjoy epic film music, jazz, singer songwriter, close harmony and much more. Who or what w

picks.title: Pure Markt
picks.blurb:
Outing for the whole family
 Get your groceries for home, take a seat on one of the terraces to taste the offer in one of the most beautiful parks in Amsterdam,

picks.title: The Maker Market
picks.blurb:
New editions, discover new creators!
 The Maker Market takes place monthly at De Hallen in Amsterdam-West, a vibrant hub of craftsmanship, design, and inspirati

picks.title: SONA presents: PAWSA In The Park
picks.blurb: Extended house set from DJ PAWSA in park setting. Sold out; resale queue active.
picks.why: Sold out daytime house in the open air

picks.title: komm schon Alter – Das Mini Festival
picks.blurb: Multi-day melodic house & techno weekend at riverside venue. 24+ crowd, curated music across both days.
picks.why: Adult-focused weekend of deep house & techno

picks.title: Syntax Error x TILLATEC
picks.blurb: Free underground party takeover. RSVP required; entry at door host's discretion.

picks.title: 't Lemmeke
picks.blurb: A country-pub-meets-bistro hybrid landing in West — Dutch cooking with village-inn warmth, opening in a week.

picks.title: Marocaine
picks.blurb: Chef Rachid El Mouhandiz, who scored a 9- in Het Parool, turns his Jordaan spot into a full ode to Moroccan cooking.

picks.title: Zuiver Wijnen
picks.blurb: Natural wine bar and bottle shop on the IJ-side quay behind Central — low-intervention bottles without the ceremony.

picks.title: Fyka
picks.blurb: The FYR team's bakery-café on the Singel: proper bread, patisserie and viennoiserie people already cycle across town for.

picks.title: Two Story
picks.venue: Two Story — gallery, design store & café
picks.blurb: An 18th-century canal house rebuilt by Copenhagen's Atelier Axo: design store up front, courtyard café, gallery rotating every three months.
picks.why: Copenhagen design eye, Amsterdam canal house

picks.title: Droog
picks.blurb: Where Dutch conceptual design was born: gallery, shop, courtyard garden and a single hotel room in a Staalstraat warehouse.

picks.title: MENDO
picks.blurb: A self-described candy store for book aficionados: black-walled cabinet of photography, design and fashion monographs.

picks.title: Moise
picks.blurb: An understated De Pijp boutique with a razor-sharp eye — Acne, Lemaire, Marant and the like, hand-picked and beautifully shown.

picks.title: Patta
picks.blurb: The homegrown streetwear label and culture hub — drops, sneakers and the community that put Amsterdam on the map.

picks.title: Utrecht
picks.blurb: Amsterdam’s quieter little sister — wharf-side canals, the soaring Dom Tower and a real café culture, minus the crowds.

picks.title: Efteling
picks.blurb: Europe’s oldest fairytale theme park — enchanted forest, dark rides and proper coasters in a storybook world.

picks.title: IJ-Hallen
picks.blurb: Europe’s biggest flea market — 750 stalls across two NDSM shipyard warehouses, a proper treasure-hunt of vintage and junk.

picks.title: Madurodam
picks.blurb: The whole of the Netherlands at 1:25 — miniature canals, Schiphol and windmills kids can tower over.

picks.title: Muiderslot
picks.blurb: A moated medieval castle on the IJmeer — turrets, falconry and a walled garden, the best-preserved fortress in the country.

picks.title: Dappermarkt
picks.blurb: A buzzing, multicultural Oost street market — spices, textiles and world food at honest neighbourhood prices.

picks.title: Bloemenmarkt
picks.blurb: The world’s only floating flower market — barges of tulips, bulbs and seeds moored along the Singel since 1862.

picks.title: Wils at the Farm
picks.blurb: Joris Bijdendijk and Thomas Val cook open-fire dinners at the farm that grows their ingredients — long tables, meadow views.
picks.why: Star kitchen relocated to its farm

picks.title: Marken & Volendam
picks.blurb: Two former Zuiderzee fishing villages — wooden houses, working harbours and smoked eel, an easy half-day from Centraal.

picks.title: Zandvoort Beach
picks.blurb: The nearest North Sea beach — wide sands, beach clubs and a direct train from Centraal; in summer half the city decamps here.

picks.title: Albert Cuypmarkt
picks.blurb: The Netherlands’ biggest street market — 250-odd stalls of stroopwafels, cheese, fabric and flowers the length of De Pijp.

picks.title: Waterlooplein Flea Market
picks.blurb: The city’s oldest flea market — secondhand everything, vintage denim, records and bric-a-brac spread out beside the Stopera.

picks.title: Cinema Culinair
picks.blurb: Watch films like Chef while eating dishes from the screen—brunch or seven-course dinner timed to scenes.
picks.why: Unique culinary film experience with synchronized meals

picks.title: BRET Club Night: Ciel, Jesse Maas, Julian Anthony, Caro
picks.blurb: House and techno in shipping-container venue. Industrial, red-walled club on Amsterdam's nightlife edge.

picks.title: American Identities
picks.blurb:

picks.title: Martine Gutierrez – Wunderkind
picks.blurb: Personal journey through identity and self-expression: new photos, self-portraits, childhood toys, handmade objects explore becoming ourselves.

picks.title: Ekō – Japan in twee beeldverhalen
picks.blurb: Japanese visual narratives explored through museum's maritime connection; paintings and historical perspectives on Japan's cultural exchanges.

picks.title: Nederland – Japan. Door de tijd verbonden
picks.blurb: Explore Japan-Netherlands cultural exchange through rare Japanese art, ceramics, swords, and lacquer boxes in the Royal Palace.

picks.title: Nicolas van Poucke - Pianorecital
picks.blurb:

picks.title: Stoornis of my life
picks.blurb: Musical starring Alex Klaasen about life, autism, loss and resilience with live music, glitter and heartfelt storytelling.
picks.why: Hilarious, touching musical with live music

picks.title: The Little Big Things
picks.blurb: West End musical based on true story showing how one moment changes everything, with live music and emotional storytelling.
picks.why: Award-winning West End musical in iconic venue

picks.title: Agatha's Almanac
picks.blurb:
Life on the farm in pictures

picks.blurb:

picks.blurb:

picks.title: VBX Summer Weekend
picks.blurb: Rey Colino, Paquita Gordon, Muallem, Ion Ludwig and more at TILLATEC.

picks.title: PRNCPTL DOES AMSTERDAM - DAY & NIGHT
picks.blurb: CRYME, DJ Fuckoff, SALOME, Slimfit and more at Lofi.

picks.title: Garlic Festival at Restaurant VRR
picks.blurb: Market with garlic products and three-course dinner celebrating garlic as the star ingredient.

picks.blurb:
Bottomless brunch

picks.title: Jazz @ H'ART Museum
picks.blurb:
Wonderful jazz in a summer setting
 Jazz lovers can count on a varied program performed by talents from the Amsterdam Conservatory. From energetic saxophone set

picks.blurb:
Accessible stage
 Open Podium Noord is free to attend. This makes the event accessible to everyone. The performances vary each week, depending on the artists wh

picks.title: Amsterdam Yard Sale Braderie
picks.blurb: Neighborhood flea market: 100 stalls of locals selling vintage items, clothes, curiosities.

picks.blurb:

picks.title: BAK
picks.blurb: A light-filled warehouse with an open kitchen and an art-covered wall; a set seasonal menu of produce-driven dishes.
